title: Malignant otitis externa (Pseudomonas)
vignette: Elderly diabetic with severe otitis externa, granulation tissue in the canal, Gram-negative rod on culture. Best microbiological description of the organism?
whatMatters: Pseudomonas = aerobic, motile, oxidase-positive, NON-lactose-fermenting GNR (pyocyanin, grape odor).
strategy: Name the bug from the script, then recall its lab fingerprint; when an answer has two clauses, BOTH must fit.

eliminations.correctIf: Patient had seawater exposure or ate raw oysters and developed rapidly spreading necrotizing cellulitis (Vibrio)
Organism was a lactose-fermenting Gram-negative rod in a UTI or pneumonia context

eliminations.what: Right on lactose, wrong on motility.
eliminations.correctIf: Organism was non-motile AND lactose-negative (Acinetobacter or Stenotrophomonas scenario)
Child had acute otitis media or an unvaccinated adult had epiglottitis (Haemophilus influenzae)

reasoning: Diabetic + necrotizing otitis externa + GNR = Pseudomonas
Pseudomonas: aerobic, motile, oxidase+, non-lactose fermenter
mechanism: Pseudomonas aeruginosa: aerobic, motile, oxidase+, non-lactose-fermenting GNR; pyocyanin (blue-green), grape odor, exotoxin A (inactivates EF-2). Untreated MOE can erode into skull-base osteomyelitis + cranial nerve palsies.

firstAid.location: Micro > gram-negative rods; malignant otitis externa

memoryHook: AERuginosa = AERobic, oxidase-positive, blue-green and fruity.
